title: Code should not force garbage collection
messageFormat: Remove this call to GC.Collect and let the runtime manage collection.
summary: An explicit GC.Collect forces a full collection on the runtime's schedule rather than its own. It almost always hurts: it promotes short-lived objects, stalls threads, and masks the real allocation problem instead of fixing it.
Remove the call and address the underlying allocation pattern; the garbage collector is tuned to decide when collection is worthwhile.
